flags.description: Master toggle for new-SA creation. Off = the platform-admin surface refuses POST with 503. Ops-only — used during migration windows OR active-incident lockdown.

Master toggle for the token-exchange endpoint. Off = every exchange refuses with 503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE. Existing JWTs signed before the flip continue to verify until natural expiry — the flip breaks re-issuance, not existing traffic. Reserved for KMS or auth-module incidents.

Off = service_account.verify_jwt middleware ALWAYS passes. RESERVED for catastrophic incident recovery only — flipping this off means every SA JWT is trusted without signature verification. Requires two-person sign-off + writes a compliance-critical audit entry.
Off = NotifyRotationDueJob is a no-op. Reserved for platform-wide notification pauses (e.g. an incident on the mail relay).
Off = DetectDormantServiceAccountJob is a no-op. Reserved for post-migration windows where dormant SAs are expected to spike (bulk-provisioned test fleet).

Off = ServiceAccountFingerprinter continues to record observations but never dispatches SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification. Used when the notification is generating false positives (e.g. new deployment IP subnet not yet added to the baseline).

Off = `service_accounts_ip_allowlist` entitlement is honoured in metadata but NOT enforced at request time. Flip ON when Wave 1b's audit-middleware upgrade lands. Documented in Wave 1a with `default: off` so the enterprise-tier entitlement is available for planning without breaking pre-Wave 1b tests.
Off = token-exchange rate-limit stays at base thresholds regardless of consecutive failures. Used for chaos testing OR when investigating a rate-limit-related regression.

Off = service_account.audit middleware is a no-op. RESERVED for emergencies where audit-log writes are overwhelming the DB. Flipping this off breaches SOC 2 CC6.1; requires compliance sign-off + a paired ticket to restore.
